subject: [RFC PATCH v2 3/7] mm, swap: support physical swap as a vswap backend

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged Yosry Ahmed's feedback about the swapoff path needing to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreeing that the logic is correct and no change is needed.

Author acknowledged a potential issue with erasing swap entries being asymmetric between vswap and non-vswap cases, but didn't commit to addressing it.
reviews.raw_body:
Yup that's the right logic. We already change the backend to physical
swap slot here, so there's no real "erase".

---

I'm not entirely sure if its worth it either, yeah. Unlike load and
store, erase seems a bit asymmetric in the sense that we only need to
do it for non-vswap cases.

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yosry Ahmed expressed mild concern about the lack of an erase operation in the vswap case, suggesting that a comment explaining this omission would be helpful.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah :/

Maybe just add a comment why no erase is needed for the vswap case.

2026-06-24.patch_summary: This patch adds support for physical swap as a backend for the virtual swap layer (vswap). It allows vswap to allocate a physical slot on demand when needed, serving as a fallback for zswap_store failures or as the destination for zswap writeback. The physical slot is tracked via a Pointer-tagged swap_table entry on the physical cluster, referencing the vswap entry. This patch modifies various kernel files, including mm/memory.c, mm/page_io.c, and mm/vmscan.c, to enable this functionality.
